title: L003: Add operator resilience and workload chaos tests
summary: Implements 3 chaos E2E test scenarios (SC-CHAOS-004, SC-CHAOS-005, SC-CHAOS-008) covering operator self-recovery after pod crash, CronJob fernet rotation failure tolerance, and PDB availability guarantee during API pod kill. Each scenario has 3 files (Keystone CR fixture, PodChaos CR, chainsaw test) totaling 9 new test files. Reference documentation updated with all 3 scenarios including inventory table, detailed sections, test patterns, PodChaos CRD patterns, fixtures table, file layout, and diagnostics matrix.

tests_checklist.item: All 9 test files exist (3 directories x 3 files)
SPDX Apache-2.0 headers on all 9 files (lines 1-3)
CC-0048 references in comment blocks on all files
Unique CR name/database pairs: keystone-chaos-op/keystone_chaos_op, keystone-chaos-cron/keystone_chaos_cron, keystone-chaos-api/keystone_chaos_api
No name collision with L002 CRs (keystone-chaos-db, keystone-chaos-mc, keystone-chaos-bao)
SC-CHAOS-004: pod-kill action, selector targets keystone-operator
SC-CHAOS-005: pod-failure action with duration: 60s, selector targets job-name: chaos-cron-test
SC-CHAOS-008: pod-kill action, multi-label selector (name + instance)
All chainsaw tests use numbered step pattern and JMESPath condition assertions
Catch blocks present on all assert steps across all 3 tests
SC-CHAOS-004: condition-based waits (Ready=false then Ready=true) on operator pod
SC-CHAOS-005: PodChaos applied BEFORE Job creation (Step 3 before Step 4)
SC-CHAOS-005: asserts FernetKeysReady=True and Ready=True maintained
SC-CHAOS-008: PDB assertion on keystone-chaos-api-api with minAvailable: 1 (policy/v1)
SC-CHAOS-008: script verifies availableReplicas >= 1 during pod kill

code_quality_checklist.item: Follows existing chaos E2E test suite structure pattern exactly
CR fixtures follow identical spec pattern to mariadb-pod-kill/00-keystone-cr.yaml
PodChaos CRDs follow established structure with correct fields
Step numbering and comment format consistent with L002 tests
diagnostics.sh usage consistent across all catch blocks

security_checklist.item: No secrets or credentials in test fixtures
No path traversal or injection risks in script steps
PodChaos selectors correctly scoped to avoid unintended targets

architecture_checklist.item: Each scenario tests a distinct resilience concern (operator lifecycle, workload failure, availability guarantee)
Test patterns appropriate for each scenario type
File layout follows established conventions (00-/01- prefix, directory per scenario)

dry_yagni_checklist.item: No duplicated test logic — each scenario is unique
Shared diagnostics.sh reused instead of duplicating inline kubectl commands
No speculative features or unused test steps

fail_fast_checklist.item: Script step exits 1 on PDB enforcement failure with descriptive error
Catch blocks collect diagnostics on every assert failure

defensive_checklist.item: Polling script uses ${READY:-0} and ${AVAILABLE:-0} defaults for empty values
PodChaos cleanup (delete) before recovery assertion in all scenarios
Condition-based waits instead of fixed sleeps for flakiness resistance

issues_found.description: The polling script in api-pod-kill-pdb Step 5 is a multi-command script that omits 'set -euo pipefail'. The chainsaw E2E test pattern says 'Script steps use set -euo pipefail for multi-command scripts'. Here the omission is justified (polling loop with defensive defaults would break under set -e), but a comment explaining the intentional omission would improve clarity.
issues_found.fix: Add a comment at the top of the script block: '# NOTE: set -euo pipefail intentionally omitted — polling loop uses defensive defaults (${READY:-0}) to handle transient kubectl failures gracefully.'
issues_found.evidence:
content: |
  # Poll until readyReplicas < 3 (confirms pod kill took effect)
  for i in $(seq 1 60); do
    READY=$(kubectl get deployment ...)

suggested_improvements: Consider adding a comment in the api-pod-kill-pdb chainsaw-test.yaml noting that PDB enforcement is validated via spec assertion (Step 3) while the script step (Step 5) validates availability maintenance during involuntary disruption — PDB only governs voluntary disruptions (evictions/drains), so the two validations are complementary rather than redundant.
next_steps: Rebase onto latest main before PR to resolve the CC-0072 feature file divergence (file added to main after branch creation)
